edges.description: Combined NEU1/GLB1 deficiency impairs glycoconjugate degradation, so substrates accumulate.
edges.source: Cathepsin A (PPCA) Deficiency and Secondary Combined NEU1/GLB1 Deficiency

edges.description: Lysosomal storage drives progressive multisystem and neurological disease.
intermediate_mechanisms: Glycoprotein and glycolipid catabolic failure with tissue storage.

edges.description: Ocular storage disease produces the macular cherry-red spot that characterizes galactosialidosis.
edges.target: Cherry red spot of the macula

edges.description: Multisystem lysosomal storage produces coarse facies.
The multisystem storage phenotype includes dysostosis multiplex and other skeletal dysplasia features.
Skeletal storage disease includes vertebral morphology abnormalities.
Visceral storage disease is associated with hepatosplenomegaly in the severe infantile spectrum.
Neurologic storage disease is associated with intellectual disability.
Storage-related neurologic and otologic involvement includes hearing impairment.
Neurologic involvement in galactosialidosis includes seizures.
Ocular storage involvement includes corneal opacity.

edges.target: Cathepsin A (PPCA) Deficiency and Secondary Combined NEU1/GLB1 Deficiency

nodes.description: Vertebral morphology abnormalities are recorded as very frequent in Orphanet.

evidence.snippet: HP:0003468 | Abnormal vertebral morphology | Very frequent (99-80%)

meta.association: Biallelic CTSA variants abolishing protective protein/cathepsin A (PPCA)

evidence.reference_title: Galactosialidosis: review and analysis of CTSA gene mutations.
evidence.snippet:
Mutations in the CTSA gene, that encodes the protective
protein/cathepsin A or PPCA, lead to the secondary deficiency of β-galactosidase
(GLB1) and neuraminidase 1 (NEU1), causing the lysosomal storage disorder
galactosialidosis (GS).

nodes.description: Biallelic CTSA variants abolish protective protein/cathepsin A (PPCA), required to assemble, stabilize, and activate the lysosomal multienzyme complex containing neuraminidase-1 and beta-galactosidase. PPCA loss secondarily inactivates both enzymes.
nodes.id: Cathepsin A (PPCA) Deficiency and Secondary Combined NEU1/GLB1 Deficiency

evidence.reference_title: Galactosialidosis: review and analysis of CTSA gene mutations.
evidence.snippet:
Mutations in the CTSA gene, that encodes the protective
protein/cathepsin A or PPCA, lead to the secondary deficiency of β-galactosidase
(GLB1) and neuraminidase 1 (NEU1), causing the lysosomal storage disorder
galactosialidosis (GS).
Genetic defects in NEU1 or in its protective protein cathepsin A (PPCA, CTSA) cause the lysosomal storage diseases sialidosis and galactosialidosis

nodes.description: A macular cherry-red spot is a characteristic ophthalmologic finding.
nodes.id: Cherry red spot of the macula

evidence.snippet: HP:0010729 | Cherry red spot of the macula | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Coarse facies develop with somatic storage.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000280 | Coarse facial features | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Corneal opacity is a very frequent ocular phenotype.
Dysostosis multiplex (skeletal dysplasia) is a characteristic somatic feature.

evidence.reference_title: Pathogenesis, Emerging therapeutic targets and Treatment in Sialidosis.
evidence.snippet: coarse face, enlargement of spleen and liver, dysostosis multiplex, vertebral deformities, and severe mental retardation

nodes.description: Hearing impairment is a very frequent sensory phenotype in the Orphanet record.
Hepatosplenomegaly reflects visceral storage, prominent in the infantile forms.

evidence.reference_title: Pathogenesis, Emerging therapeutic targets and Treatment in Sialidosis.
evidence.snippet: clinical presentation at birth includes facial edema, inguinal hernias and hepatosplenomegaly

nodes.description: Intellectual disability occurs in the severe and juvenile/adult forms.
Storage-cell injury produces progressive multisystem disease (skeletal, ocular, cardiac, renal, and neurological), most severe in the early-infantile form, completing the lysosomal-storage cascade.

evidence.snippet: the rare infantile form of GS

nodes.description: Seizures are recorded as a very frequent neurologic phenotype.

Secondary combined deficiency of neuraminidase-1 and beta-galactosidase impairs glycoprotein and glycolipid catabolism, causing intralysosomal accumulation and urinary excretion of sialylated oligosaccharides and glycopeptides.
